```{r}
    y = "Climate distance (standardized)",
    title = "Climate distance vs. geographic distance for matched pairs"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

## without replacement
```{r}

without_replacement <- function(dist_mat, clim_mat, geo_mat, n_match = 5) {
  
  # Build a long list of every possible (burned, control) pair with its distances
  triples <- expand.grid(
    burned_id  = rownames(dist_mat),
    control_id = colnames(dist_mat),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  triples$combined_distance <- as.vector(dist_mat)
  triples$climate_distance  <- as.vector(clim_mat)
  triples$geo_distance_km   <- as.vector(geo_mat)
  
  # Sort so the best (smallest distance) pairs come first
  triples <- triples[order(triples$combined_distance), ]
  
  needed   <- setNames(rep(n_match, nrow(dist_mat)), rownames(dist_mat))
  used_ctl <- setNames(rep(FALSE, ncol(dist_mat)), colnames(dist_mat))
  
  results <- vector("list", nrow(dist_mat) * n_match)
  counter <- 1
  
  for (k in seq_len(nrow(triples))) {
    b <- triples$burned_id[k]
    c <- triples$control_id[k]
    
    if (needed[b] > 0 && !used_ctl[c]) {
      results[[counter]] <- data.frame(
        burned_id         = b,
        control_id        = c,
        combined_distance = triples$combined_distance[k],
        climate_distance  = triples$climate_distance[k],
        geo_distance_km   = triples$geo_distance_km[k],
        rank              = n_match - needed[b] + 1,
        row.names         = NULL
      )
      counter <- counter + 1
      needed[b]   <- needed[b] - 1
      used_ctl[c] <- TRUE
    }
    
    if (all(needed == 0)) break
  }
  
  bind_rows(results)
}

OR_matches_no_replacement <- without_replacement(
  dist_mat = OR_combined_dist,
  clim_mat = OR_climate_distance_matrix,
  geo_mat  = OR_geo_dist_mat,
  n_match  = 3
)

OR_matches_no_replacement %>% count(burned_id) %>% filter(n < 5)
```


## species overlap
```{r}
# now that we have our control and burned plot pairs, lets look into species overlap between each pair

OR_burned_and_control_plot_id <- unique(c(OR_matches_no_replacement$burned_id, OR_matches_no_replacement$control_id))

#2212 plots total
# burned
# control

# lets filter CA_TREE and CA_SEEDLING for our burned and control plots

OR_TREE_IDs_filtered <- OR_TREE_IDs %>%
  filter(plot_id %in% OR_burned_and_control_plot_id)


# add species names to species codes using REF_SPECIES

OR_TREE_species <- OR_TREE_IDs_filtered %>%
  left_join(
    REF_SPECIES %>%
      mutate(species = paste(GENUS, SPECIES, sep = "_")) %>%
      select(SPCD, species),
    by = "SPCD"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    OR_COND_treatment %>% distinct(plot_id, condition),
    by = "plot_id"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    OR_prepost_fire_summary %>% distinct(plot_id, fire_year),
    by = "plot_id"
  )

OR_TREE_species_filtered <- OR_TREE_species %>%
  filter(
    condition == "burned" | condition == "control"
  )

#Compute total abundance per species — count all tree rows per species, across every plot.
#Compute frequency (commonness) per species — count the number of distinct plots each species appears in, divide by total number of plots.

# for all plots
OR_species_summary_combined <- OR_TREE_species_filtered %>%
  group_by(species) %>%
  summarise(
    total_abundance = n(),
    n_plots = n_distinct(plot_id),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    frequency = n_plots / n_distinct(OR_TREE_species_filtered$plot_id)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_abundance))

# by condition
OR_species_summary_by_condition <- OR_TREE_species_filtered %>%
  group_by(condition, species) %>%
  summarise(
    total_abundance = n(),
    n_plots = n_distinct(plot_id),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  mutate(
    frequency = n_plots / n_distinct(OR_TREE_species_filtered$plot_id[OR_TREE_species_filtered$condition == condition])
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(condition, desc(total_abundance))


# Rank species on each metric separately, then combine the two ranks into one overall score (e.g., average rank, so a species that's #3 in abundance and #10 in frequency gets averaged into a combined rank).

#Sort by the combined rank and take the top 75.

OR_top_75_species <- OR_species_summary_combined %>%
  mutate(
    abundance_rank = rank(-total_abundance),
    frequency_rank = rank(-frequency),
    combined_rank = (abundance_rank + frequency_rank) / 2
  ) %>%
  arrange(combined_rank) %>%
  slice_head(n = 75)
# 54 species total

#total number of species across both control and burned plots
OR_TREE_species_filtered %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  summarise(n_species = n_distinct(species), .groups = "drop")

OR_all_species <- OR_TREE_species_filtered %>%
  distinct(species) %>%
  pull(species)

length(OR_all_species)
# 54 total species

# to calculate dominance (the number of indiviudals of that species in that plot) and frequency (commonness)  (the number of plots the species occurs in / total number of plots)

OR_tree_count <- OR_TREE_species_filtered %>%
  group_by(plot_id) %>%
  summarise(total_trees_in_plot = n(), .groups = "drop")

OR_species_dominance <- OR_TREE_species_filtered %>%
  group_by(plot_id, species) %>%
  summarise(species_count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(OR_tree_count, by = "plot_id") %>%
  mutate(relative_abundance = species_count / total_trees_in_plot)

OR_species_summary <- OR_species_dominance %>%
  group_by(species) %>%
  summarise(
    n_plots_present  = n_distinct(plot_id),
    avg_dominance    = mean(relative_abundance),
    median_dominance = median(relative_abundance),
    min_dominance    = min(relative_abundance),
    max_dominance    = max(relative_abundance),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(frequency = n_plots_present / n_distinct(OR_TREE_species_filtered$plot_id)) %>%
  arrange(desc(frequency))

# lets check whether dominant and common species differ by condition (burned vs control)

OR_species_dominance_condition <- OR_species_dominance %>%
  left_join(
    OR_TREE_species_filtered %>% distinct(plot_id, condition),
    by = "plot_id"
  )

# now summarize by condition + species instead of species alone
OR_species_summary_by_condition <- OR_species_dominance_condition %>%
  group_by(condition, species) %>%
  summarise(
    n_plots_present  = n_distinct(plot_id),
    avg_dominance    = mean(relative_abundance),
    median_dominance = median(relative_abundance),
    min_dominance    = min(relative_abundance),
    max_dominance    = max(relative_abundance),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  mutate(
    frequency = n_plots_present / n_distinct(OR_TREE_species_filtered$plot_id[OR_TREE_species_filtered$condition == condition])
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(condition, desc(frequency))

OR_species_condition_comparison <- OR_species_summary_by_condition %>%
  select(condition, species, frequency, median_dominance) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = condition,
    values_from = c(frequency, median_dominance)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    frequency_diff = frequency_burned - frequency_control,
    dominance_diff = median_dominance_burned - median_dominance_control
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(dominance_diff)))

library(ggrepel)

OR_species_condition_comparison %>%
  filter(!is.na(dominance_diff), !is.na(frequency_diff)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = frequency_diff, y = dominance_diff, label = species)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray70") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray70") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3, max.overlaps = 15) +
  labs(
    x = "Frequency difference (burned − control)",
    y = "Dominance difference (burned − control)",
    title = "Burned - control species dominance (median)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# is the number of species recruiting in control plots greater than burned (OR)

OR_SEEDLING_condition_filtered <- OR_SEEDLING_IDs %>%
  filter(plot_id %in% OR_burned_and_control_plot_id) %>%
  left_join(
    OR_COND_treatment %>% distinct(plot_id, condition),
    by = "plot_id"
  )

OR_SEEDLING_species_summary_condition <- OR_SEEDLING_condition_filtered %>%
  group_by(plot_id, condition, SPCD) %>%
  summarise(n_seedlings = sum(TREECOUNT, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(REF_SPECIES %>% dplyr::select(SPCD, GENUS, SPECIES), by = "SPCD") %>%
  mutate(species = paste(GENUS, SPECIES, sep = "_"))

OR_SEEDLING_plot_summary_condition <- OR_SEEDLING_species_summary_condition %>%
  group_by(plot_id, condition) %>%
  summarise(
    n_seedling_species = n_distinct(SPCD),
    n_seedlings = sum(n_seedlings, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

OR_SEEDLING_plot_summary_condition %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n_seedling_species, fill = condition)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, color = "white", position = "identity", alpha = 0.6) +
  facet_wrap(~condition, ncol = 1) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "Number of seedling species per plot",
    y = "Number of plots",
    title = "Seedling species richness: burned vs. control (OR)"
  )

OR_SEEDLING_plot_summary_condition %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = condition, y = n_seedling_species, fill = condition)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Number of seedling species per plot",
    title = "Seedling species richness: burned vs. control (OR)"
  )

OR_seedling_richness_summary_by_condition <- OR_SEEDLING_plot_summary_condition %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_richness   = mean(n_seedling_species),
    median_richness = median(n_seedling_species),
    n_plots         = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

OR_seedling_richness_summary_by_condition

OR_SEEDLING_species_summary_condition %>%
  group_by(species, condition, plot_id) %>%
  summarise(n_seedlings = sum(n_seedlings), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(species, condition) %>%
  summarise(mean_per_plot = mean(n_seedlings), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  slice_max(mean_per_plot, n = 20) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder_within(species, mean_per_plot, condition), y = mean_per_plot, fill = condition)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_x_reordered() +
  facet_wrap(~condition, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = "species",
    y = "average # seedlings per plot",
    title = "Average seedling abundance per species per plot: burned vs. control (OR)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

OR_SEEDLING_species_summary_condition %>%
  group_by(plot_id, condition, species) %>%
  summarise(n_seedlings = sum(n_seedlings), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(n_seedlings_capped = pmin(n_seedlings, 50)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = species, y = n_seedlings_capped, fill = condition)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6) +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_wrap(~condition, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = "species",
    y = "seedlings per plot (capped at 50)",
    title = "Seedling abundance per species per plot: burned vs. control (OR)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

summary(OR_SEEDLING_plot_summary_condition$n_seedling_species[OR_SEEDLING_plot_summary_condition$condition == "burned"])
summary(OR_SEEDLING_plot_summary_condition$n_seedling_species[OR_SEEDLING_plot_summary_condition$condition == "control"])

```

```{r}

# species found in both states
species_shared_CA_OR <- intersect(CA_all_species, OR_all_species)
length(species_shared_CA_OR)

# species found only in CA
species_only_CA <- setdiff(CA_all_species, OR_all_species)
length(species_only_CA)

# species found only in OR
species_only_OR <- setdiff(OR_all_species, CA_all_species)
length(species_only_OR)

# sanity check: total unique species across both states combined
length(union(CA_all_species, OR_all_species))
```

## whole plot network
```{r}

# filter CA_COND_IDs for forest types only, no disturbance filtering
OR_COND_forest_type <- OR_COND_IDs %>%
  filter(FORTYPCD %in% c(201, 202, 221, 222, 224, 225, 226, 241, 261, 262, 263, 264, 265, 267, 268, 270, 271, 281, 301, 304, 305, 321, 341, 342, 361, 365, 371, 722, 911, 912, 922, 923, 933, 941, 961))
         
(371, 201, 221, 222, 224, 225, 261, 922))

# get the unique plot_ids that qualify
OR_forest_type_plot_id <- OR_COND_forest_type %>%
  distinct(plot_id) %>%
  pull(plot_id)

length(OR_forest_type_plot_id)

# filter CA_PLOT_IDs for those plot_ids
OR_PLOT_forest_type <- OR_PLOT_IDs %>%
  filter(plot_id %in% OR_forest_type_plot_id)

# filter CA_TREE_IDs for those plot_ids
OR_TREE_forest_type <- OR_TREE_IDs %>%
  filter(plot_id %in% OR_forest_type_plot_id)

nrow(OR_PLOT_forest_type)
n_distinct(OR_TREE_forest_type$plot_id)

OR_TREE_forest_type_species <- OR_TREE_forest_type %>%
  left_join(
    REF_SPECIES %>%
      mutate(species = paste(GENUS, SPECIES, sep = "_")) %>%
      select(SPCD, species),
    by = "SPCD"
  )
sum(is.na(OR_TREE_forest_type_species$species))
n_distinct(OR_TREE_forest_type_species$species)

# how different are the species from this workflow and the previous control plot filtering by climate?

# species from your burned/control workflow (already built)
OR_all_species   # from earlier: distinct species across CA_TREE_species_filtered

# species from your new forest-type-only workflow
OR_forest_type_all_species <- OR_TREE_forest_type_species %>%
  distinct(species) %>%
  pull(species)

length(OR_forest_type_all_species)

# species found in both workflows
intersect(OR_all_species, OR_forest_type_all_species)

# species found only in the burned/control workflow
setdiff(OR_all_species, OR_forest_type_all_species)

# species found only in the forest-type-only workflow
setdiff(OR_forest_type_all_species, OR_all_species)


# make sure the species coming out of SEEDLING match TREE
# filter CA_SEEDLING_IDs for those plot_ids
OR_SEEDLING_forest_type <- OR_SEEDLING_IDs %>%
  filter(plot_id %in% OR_forest_type_plot_id)

OR_SEEDLING_forest_type_species <- OR_SEEDLING_forest_type %>%
  left_join(
    REF_SPECIES %>%
      mutate(species = paste(GENUS, SPECIES, sep = "_")) %>%
      select(SPCD, species),
    by = "SPCD"
  )

# species list from seedling data (forest-type-only workflow)
OR_forest_type_all_seedling_species <- OR_SEEDLING_forest_type_species %>%
  distinct(species) %>%
  pull(species)

length(OR_forest_type_all_species)
length(OR_forest_type_all_seedling_species)

# species found in both tree and seedling data
intersect(OR_forest_type_all_species, OR_forest_type_all_seedling_species)

# species found only in tree data (mature trees, no seedlings recorded)
setdiff(OR_forest_type_all_species, OR_forest_type_all_seedling_species)

# species found only in seedling data (recruiting, but no mature trees recorded)
setdiff(OR_forest_type_all_seedling_species, OR_forest_type_all_species)

# create a shared species list
OR_forest_type_all_species
OR_forest_type_all_seedling_species

OR_species <- data.frame(
  species = unique(c(
    OR_forest_type_all_species,
    OR_forest_type_all_seedling_species
  ))
)

# how much overlap is there between CA and OR all species for our forest types
setdiff(OR_species, CA_species) # 18 species in OR only 
setdiff(CA_species, OR_species) # 22 species in CA only 

# Number of shared species
length(intersect(CA_species$species, OR_species$species))

# Species only in Oregon
length(setdiff(OR_species$species, CA_species$species))   # 18

# Species only in California
length(setdiff(CA_species$species, OR_species$species))   # 22

# Total unique species across both states
length(union(CA_species$species, OR_species$species)) 
# 95 unique species across both states

# Or equivalently
length(unique(c(CA_species$species, OR_species$species)))

```


#WA
### fire only plots
```{r}
#create a unique PLOT ID in COND and in PLOT tables 
WA_COND_IDs <- WA_COND %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    plot_id = paste(STATECD, UNITCD, COUNTYCD, PLOT, sep = "_")
  )
WA_PLOT_IDs <- WA_PLOT %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    plot_id = paste(STATECD, UNITCD, COUNTYCD, PLOT, sep = "_")
  )

# collapse information from multiple CONDIDs associated with each plot to avoid many-to-many mapping
# and then filter by fire and forest type  

#plot_id → multiple CONDIDs → each CONDID has up to 3 disturbance codes and years
# one row for a specific plot_id, CONID, and disturbance event (combining DSTRBCD and DSTRBYR)
WA_COND_long <- WA_COND_IDs %>%
  dplyr::select(plot_id, CONDID, FORTYPCD,
    DSTRBCD1, DSTRBCD2, DSTRBCD3,
    DSTRBYR1, DSTRBYR2, DSTRBYR3) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("DSTR"),
    names_to = c(".value", "event"),
    names_pattern = "(DSTRBCD|DSTRBYR)([1-3])"
  ) %>%
  filter(DSTRBCD %in% c(30, 31, 32)) %>%
  filter(FORTYPCD %in% c(371, 201, 221, 222, 224, 225, 261, 922)) %>%
  mutate(
    fire_year_severity = paste(DSTRBYR, DSTRBCD, sep = "_")
  )

# summarize fire history across rows for unique plot_id (one row per plot_id)
WA_fire_summary <- WA_COND_long %>%
  group_by(plot_id) %>%
  summarise(
    n_fire_events = n_distinct(DSTRBYR), # number of distinct fire years per plot
    
    # this just collapses all codes into a list but not necessarily relative to year
    fire_types = paste(sort(unique(DSTRBCD)), collapse = ", "), 
    fire_years = paste(sort(unique(DSTRBYR)), collapse = ", "),
    
    fire_year_severity =
    paste(unique(fire_year_severity), collapse = ", "),
    
    multiple_fires = n_distinct(DSTRBYR) > 1, # did fire occur more than once?
    multiple_severity = n_distinct(DSTRBCD) > 1, # are there multiple fire types?
    first_fire_year = min(DSTRBYR, na.rm = TRUE),
    last_fire_year = max(DSTRBYR, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# how many plots burned multiple times?
WA_fire_summary %>% summarise(n_plots_multiple_fires = sum(multiple_fires))
# 4 plots

# how many plots that burned multipe times have different severities?
WA_fire_summary %>% filter(multiple_fires) %>% summarise(n_plots_multiple_fire_and_severity = sum(multiple_severity))
# 0 plots

# how many plots that burned once have multiple severities within a plot?
WA_fire_summary %>%
  filter(!multiple_fires) %>%
  summarise(
    n_plots_single_fire_multiple_severity =
      sum(multiple_severity))
# 5 plots

WA_burned_PLOT_IDs <- WA_fire_summary %>% 
  distinct(plot_id)

# merge COND and PLOT by this unique ID 
WA_PLOT_by_COND <- WA_PLOT_IDs %>%
  semi_join(WA_burned_PLOT_IDs, by = "plot_id") %>%  #keep only plot_ids with at least one fire 
  left_join(WA_fire_summary, by = "plot_id")  #add fire summary to each plot, should be one-to-many mapping

#for each unique plot, count how many unique inventory years (INVYR) it has
WA_PLOT_by_COND %>%
  group_by(plot_id) %>%
  summarise(
    n_revisits = n_distinct(INVYR),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  count(n_revisits)

# n=2 (241)


# how many plots have pre and post fire surveys
WA_fire_events <- WA_COND_long %>%
  dplyr::select(plot_id, DSTRBYR) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  rename(fire_year = DSTRBYR)

# does each unique plot_id have a pre and post fire survey around any/all fire years?
WA_prepost_fire_summary <- WA_PLOT_by_COND %>%
  distinct(plot_id, INVYR) %>%
  inner_join(
    WA_fire_events %>% distinct(plot_id, fire_year),
    by = "plot_id"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    relationship = case_when(
      INVYR < fire_year ~ "pre",
      INVYR > fire_year ~ "post",
      TRUE ~ "same_year"
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(plot_id, fire_year) %>%
  summarise(
    has_pre  = any(relationship == "pre"),
    has_post = any(relationship == "post"),
    n_pre_surveys  = sum(relationship == "pre"),
    n_post_surveys = sum(relationship == "post"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
      status = case_when(
      has_pre & has_post ~ "pre_and_post",
      has_pre ~ "pre_only",
      has_post ~ "post_only",
      TRUE ~ "no_clear_separation"
    )
  )

WA_prepost_fire_summary %>%
  count(status)

# post only = 79
# pre only = 11
#pre and post = 155


# how many plots are there in CA
WA_PLOT %>%
  distinct(LAT, LON) %>%
  nrow()
# 11778

length(unique(WA_COND_IDs$plot_id)) # 11988
length(unique(WA_PLOT_IDs$plot_id)) # 11988


```
## tree
```{r}
#Tree number. A number used to uniquely identify a tree on a subplot. Tree numbers can be used to track trees when PLOT.DESIGNCD or DESIGNCD is the same between inventories

# before looking into tree level data, just confirm that the design codes are the same across plots in our CA_PLOT_by_COND table that has our burned plots
n_distinct(WA_PLOT_by_COND$DESIGNCD) # two design codes across the filtered plots
unique(WA_PLOT_by_COND$DESIGNCD) # 501, 502

# do we have surviving trees in post-fire surveys?
# create unique plot ids for CA_TREE
WA_TREE_IDs <- WA_TREE %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    plot_id = paste(STATECD, UNITCD, COUNTYCD, PLOT, sep = "_")
  )

#filter CA_TREE to only keep the unique plot ids from CA_PLOT_by_COND (our fire only plots in our focal forest types)
WA_TREE_filtered <- WA_TREE_IDs %>%
  filter(plot_id %in% WA_PLOT_by_COND$plot_id)

# to locate a specific tree ID, use the following variables 
# STATECD, INVYR, UNITCD, COUNTYCD, PLOT, SUBP, TREE
# TREE is unique to SUBP not plot_id
# we can look for unique combinations of SUBP and TREE within a plot_id (STATECD, UNITCD, COUNTYCD, PLOT)

# is TREE our unique identifier of TREE ?
WA_TREE_filtered %>%
  group_by(TREE) %>%
  summarise(
    n_plots = n_distinct(plot_id),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n_plots > 1) %>%
  summarise(
    n_trees_multiple_plots = n()
  )
# 295 trees are found in multiple plots 

WA_TREE_filtered %>%
  summarise(
    n_unique_trees = n_distinct(plot_id, SUBP, TREE)
  )
# number of unique trees 7215

# Tree number notes:
# TREE uniquely identifies a tree within a subplot (SUBP),
# so full tree identity is: plot_id + SUBP + TREE

# unique tree identity is plot_id + SUBP + TREE
# make a unique tree identifier too? or use plot_id + SUBP + TREE everytime
#tree_id = paste(plot_id, SUBP, TREE, sep = "_") 

# lets look at trees in the plots with both pre and post fire surveys
# pull the plot_ids for status = pre_and_post from CA_prepost_fire_summary and the fire year the post-fire plot is associated with

WA_TREE_prepost_fire <- WA_TREE_filtered %>%
  semi_join(
    WA_prepost_fire_summary %>% filter(status == "pre_and_post"),
    by = "plot_id"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    WA_prepost_fire_summary %>%
      filter(status == "pre_and_post") %>%
      dplyr::select(plot_id, fire_year, status),
    by = "plot_id"
  )

WA_prepost_fire_summary %>%
  filter(status == "pre_and_post") %>%
  summarise(n_plots = n_distinct(plot_id)) #155
length(unique(WA_TREE_prepost_fire$plot_id)) #155


# now we want to look at just post-fire plots (where INVYR > fire_year)
WA_TREE_post_fire_plots <- WA_TREE_prepost_fire %>%
  filter(INVYR > fire_year)

# number of trees per plot (post-fire plots)
WA_TREE_per_plot_post <- WA_TREE_post_fire_plots %>%
  filter(STATUSCD == 1) %>%
  mutate(which = "post") %>%
  group_by(plot_id, INVYR, fire_year, status, which) %>%
  summarise(
    n_live_tree = n_distinct(SUBP, TREE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
summary(WA_TREE_per_plot_post$n_live_tree)

# number of species with STATUSCD = 1 in post-fire plots
WA_TREE_post_fire_plot_summary <- WA_TREE_post_fire_plots %>%
  filter(STATUSCD == 1) %>%
  group_by(plot_id, INVYR, fire_year) %>%
  summarise(
    n_live_species = n_distinct(SPCD),
    n_live_trees = n_distinct(SUBP, TREE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
summary(WA_TREE_post_fire_plot_summary$n_live_species)

# number of individuals (live) per species per plot
WA_TREE_post_fire_species_summary <- WA_TREE_post_fire_plots %>%
  filter(STATUSCD == 1) %>%
  group_by(plot_id, INVYR, fire_year, SPCD) %>%
  summarise(
    n_live_trees = n_distinct(SUBP, TREE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    REF_SPECIES %>%
      dplyr::select(SPCD, GENUS, SPECIES),
    by = "SPCD"
  ) %>%
  mutate(species = paste(GENUS, SPECIES, sep = "_"))
summary(WA_TREE_post_fire_species_summary$n_live_trees)

## tree transitions and mortality rates

# closest PRE-fire observation for each TREE
WA_TREE_pre_fire_trees <- WA_TREE_prepost_fire %>%
  filter(INVYR < fire_year) %>%
  group_by(plot_id, SUBP, TREE, fire_year) %>%
  arrange(desc(INVYR), .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(
    pre_INVYR = INVYR,
    pre_STATUSCD = STATUSCD
  )

# closest POST-fire observation for each TREE
WA_TREE_post_fire_trees <- WA_TREE_prepost_fire %>%
  filter(INVYR > fire_year) %>%
  group_by(plot_id, SUBP, TREE, fire_year) %>%
  arrange(INVYR, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(
    post_INVYR = INVYR,
    post_STATUSCD = STATUSCD
  )

# join pre and post observations
WA_TREE_transitions <- WA_TREE_pre_fire_trees %>%
  inner_join(
    WA_TREE_post_fire_trees,
    by = c("plot_id", "SUBP", "TREE", "fire_year"),
    suffix = c("_pre", "_post")
  )

# mortality indicators
WA_TREE_transitions <- WA_TREE_transitions %>%
  mutate(
    alive_pre = pre_STATUSCD == 1,
    dead_post = pre_STATUSCD == 1 & post_STATUSCD == 2
  )

# plot-level mortality
WA_TREE_mortality <- WA_TREE_transitions %>%
  group_by(plot_id, fire_year) %>%
  summarise(
    n_alive_pre = sum(alive_pre, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_dead_post = sum(dead_post, na.rm = TRUE),
    mortality_rate = n_dead_post / n_alive_pre,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

summary(WA_TREE_mortality$mortality_rate)
hist(WA_TREE_mortality$mortality_rate)

# check for any new trees in post fire surveys

# trees appearing post-fire but absent pre-fire
WA_TREE_new_trees <- WA_TREE_post_fire_trees %>%
  anti_join(
    WA_TREE_transitions %>%
      distinct(plot_id, SUBP, fire_year, TREE),
    by = c("plot_id", "SUBP", "fire_year", "TREE")
  )

WA_TREE_new_trees %>%
  summarise(
    total_new_recruits = n()
  )
# 600 new post-fire recruits in WA_TREE

WA_TREE_new_trees %>%
  summarise(
    total_new_recruits = n_distinct(plot_id, SUBP, TREE, fire_year)
  )

# recruits per plot
WA_TREE_new_recruits_per_plot <- WA_TREE_new_trees %>%
  group_by(plot_id, fire_year, post_INVYR) %>%
  summarise(
    n_new_recruits = n_distinct(SUBP, TREE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

summary(WA_TREE_new_recruits_per_plot$n_new_recruits)

# proportion of new recruits per plot
WA_TREE_recruitment <- WA_TREE_new_recruits_per_plot %>%
  left_join(
    WA_TREE_per_plot_post %>%
      dplyr::select(
        plot_id,
        fire_year,
        post_INVYR = INVYR,
        n_live_tree
      ),
    by = c("plot_id", "fire_year", "post_INVYR")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    recruit_prop = n_new_recruits / n_live_tree
  )

WA_TREE_recruitment %>%
  filter(recruit_prop == 1)

# 1 plot has only new trees

summary(WA_TREE_recruitment$recruit_prop)
```

## seedling
```{r}
# is there recruitment in post-fire surveys?

# seedling counts are under TREECOUNT in CA_SEEDLING
# TREECOUNT is for  (plot × subplot/microplot × species × inventory)
# in the CA_SEEDLING table, SUBP = microplot since seedlings are inventoried at the microplot within a subplot

# create unique plot ids for CA_SEEDLING
WA_SEEDLING_IDs <- WA_SEEDLING %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    plot_id = paste(STATECD, UNITCD, COUNTYCD, PLOT, sep = "_")
  )

#filter CA_SEEDLING to only keep the unique plot ids from CA_PLOT_by_COND
WA_SEEDLING_filtered <- WA_SEEDLING_IDs %>%
  filter(plot_id %in% WA_PLOT_by_COND$plot_id)

# lets look at trees in the plots with both pre and post fire 
# pull the plot_ids for status = pre_and_post from CA_prepost_fire_summary

WA_SEEDLING_prepost_fire <- WA_SEEDLING_filtered %>%
  semi_join(WA_prepost_fire_summary %>% filter(status == "pre_and_post"),
    by = "plot_id") %>%
  left_join(WA_prepost_fire_summary %>%
      filter(status == "pre_and_post") %>%
      dplyr::select(plot_id, fire_year, status),
    by = "plot_id")

# filter to post-fire plots only
WA_SEEDLING_post_fire_plots <- WA_SEEDLING_prepost_fire %>%
  filter(INVYR > fire_year)


length(unique(WA_SEEDLING_post_fire_plots$plot_id)) # 68
length(unique(WA_TREE_post_fire_plots$plot_id)) # 155
# 68 unique plots (why is this different from the 155 for the TREE analysis ??)

## To qualify for counting, conifer seedlings must be at least 6 inches tall and hardwood seedlings must be at least 12 inches tall (which is maybe why that column is blank on the table).

# how many post fire plots have TREECOUNT = NA (no seedlings recorded)
WA_SEEDLING_post_fire_plots %>%
  filter(is.na(TREECOUNT)) %>%
  summarise(n_plot_ids = n_distinct(plot_id))
# 0 / 203 have no seedlings 

# number of seedlings per plot (aggregated across SUBP/microplot)
WA_SEEDLING_per_plot <- WA_SEEDLING_post_fire_plots %>%
  group_by(plot_id, INVYR, fire_year) %>%
  summarise(
    n_seedlings = sum(TREECOUNT, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

hist(WA_SEEDLING_per_plot$n_seedlings, breaks=30)
summary(WA_SEEDLING_per_plot$n_seedlings)

# number of seedlings per species per plot (aggregated across SUBP/microplot)
WA_SEEDLING_species_summary <- WA_SEEDLING_post_fire_plots %>%
  group_by(plot_id, INVYR, fire_year, SPCD) %>%
  summarise(n_seedlings = sum(TREECOUNT, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(REF_SPECIES %>% dplyr::select(SPCD, GENUS, SPECIES),
    by = "SPCD") %>%
  mutate(species = paste(GENUS, SPECIES, sep = "_"))

summary(WA_SEEDLING_species_summary$n_seedlings)


WA_SEEDLING_species_summary %>%
  group_by(plot_id) %>%
  summarise(n_species = n_distinct(SPCD)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n_species)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, fill = "darkgreen", color = "white") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "Number of seedling species per plot",
    y = "Number of plots"
  )

# number of seedling species per plot 
WA_SEEDLING_plot_summary <- WA_SEEDLING_post_fire_plots %>%
  group_by(plot_id, INVYR, fire_year) %>%
  summarise(
    n_seedling_species = n_distinct(SPCD),
    n_seedlings = sum(TREECOUNT, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

```

## expanding to include post-only
```{r}

WA_TREE_IDs <- WA_TREE %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    plot_id = paste(STATECD, UNITCD, COUNTYCD, PLOT, sep = "_")
  )

#filter CA_TREE to only keep the unique plot ids from CA_PLOT_by_COND (our fire only plots in our focal forest types)
WA_TREE_filtered <- WA_TREE_IDs %>%
  filter(plot_id %in% WA_PLOT_by_COND$plot_id)

WA_TREE_prepost_fire <- WA_TREE_filtered %>%
  semi_join(
    WA_prepost_fire_summary %>%
      filter(status %in% c("pre_and_post", "post_only")),
    by = "plot_id"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    WA_prepost_fire_summary %>%
      filter(status %in% c("pre_and_post", "post_only")) %>%
      dplyr::select(plot_id, fire_year, status),
    by = "plot_id"
  )


# now we want to look at just post-fire plots (where INVYR > fire_year)
WA_TREE_post_fire_plots <- WA_TREE_prepost_fire %>%
  filter(INVYR > fire_year)

length(unique(WA_TREE_post_fire_plots$plot_id)) # 230 post fire plots

# number of living trees per plot (post-fire plots)
WA_TREE_per_plot_post <- WA_TREE_post_fire_plots %>%
  filter(STATUSCD == 1) %>%
  mutate(which = "post") %>%
  group_by(plot_id, INVYR, fire_year, status, which) %>%
  summarise(
    n_live_tree = n_distinct(SUBP, TREE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
summary(WA_TREE_per_plot_post$n_live_tree)


# number of species with STATUSCD = 1 in post-fire plots
WA_TREE_post_fire_plot_summary <- WA_TREE_post_fire_plots %>%
  filter(STATUSCD == 1) %>%
  group_by(plot_id, INVYR, fire_year) %>%
  summarise(
    n_live_species = n_distinct(SPCD),
    n_live_trees = n_distinct(SUBP, TREE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
summary(WA_TREE_post_fire_plot_summary$n_live_species)

# number of individuals (live) per species per plot
WA_TREE_post_fire_species_summary <- WA_TREE_post_fire_plots %>%
  filter(STATUSCD == 1) %>%
  group_by(plot_id, INVYR, fire_year, SPCD) %>%
  summarise(
    n_live_trees = n_distinct(SUBP, TREE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    REF_SPECIES %>%
      dplyr::select(SPCD, GENUS, SPECIES),
    by = "SPCD"
  ) %>%
  mutate(species = paste(GENUS, SPECIES, sep = "_"))

summary(WA_TREE_post_fire_species_summary$n_live_trees)

# is there recruitment in post-fire surveys? (pre_and_post and post_only)

WA_SEEDLING_prepost_fire <- WA_SEEDLING_filtered %>%
  semi_join(WA_prepost_fire_summary %>% filter(status %in% c("pre_and_post", "post_only")),
    by = "plot_id") %>%
  left_join(WA_prepost_fire_summary %>%
      filter(status %in% c("pre_and_post", "post_only")) %>%
      dplyr::select(plot_id, fire_year, status),
    by = "plot_id")

# filter to post-fire plots only
WA_SEEDLING_post_fire_plots <- WA_SEEDLING_prepost_fire %>%
  filter(INVYR > fire_year)

length(unique(WA_SEEDLING_post_fire_plots$plot_id)) # 126
# 126 unique plots instead of the 68 in the pre_and_post analysis above

# how many post fire plots have TREECOUNT = NA (no seedlings recorded)
WA_SEEDLING_post_fire_plots %>%
  filter(is.na(TREECOUNT)) %>%
  summarise(n_plot_ids = n_distinct(plot_id))
# 4 / 126 have no seedlings 

# number of seedlings per plot (aggregated across SUBP/microplot)
WA_SEEDLING_per_plot <- WA_SEEDLING_post_fire_plots %>%
  group_by(plot_id, INVYR, fire_year) %>%
  summarise(
    n_seedlings = sum(TREECOUNT, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

hist(WA_SEEDLING_per_plot$n_seedlings, breaks=30)
summary(WA_SEEDLING_per_plot$n_seedlings)

# number of species of seedlings per plot 
WA_SEEDLING_plot_summary <- WA_SEEDLING_post_fire_plots %>%
  group_by(plot_id, INVYR, fire_year) %>%
  summarise(
    n_seedling_species = n_distinct(SPCD),
    n_seedlings = sum(TREECOUNT, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# number of individuals per species per plot
WA_SEEDLING_species_summary <- WA_SEEDLING_post_fire_plots %>%
  group_by(plot_id, INVYR, fire_year, SPCD) %>%
  summarise(n_seedlings = sum(TREECOUNT, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(REF_SPECIES %>% dplyr::select(SPCD, GENUS, SPECIES),
    by = "SPCD") %>%
  mutate(species = paste(GENUS, SPECIES, sep = "_"))
```

## control plots
```{r}
# bring in plot_ids for burned plots (pre_and_post, post_only)
WA_prepost_burned_plot_id <- WA_prepost_fire_summary %>%
  filter(status %in% c("pre_and_post", "post_only")) 

# filter for plots that have DSTRBCD == 0 in all columns (DSTRBCD1-3) and all rows
WA_COND_control_summary <- WA_COND_IDs %>%
  filter(FORTYPCD %in% c(371, 201, 221, 222, 224, 225, 261, 922)) %>%
  group_by(plot_id) %>%
  summarise(
    all_zero = all(DSTRBCD1 == 0 & DSTRBCD2 == 0 & DSTRBCD3 == 0),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(all_zero) %>%
  select(plot_id)

WA_COND_treatment <- WA_COND_IDs %>%
  filter(
    plot_id %in% WA_prepost_burned_plot_id$plot_id |
      plot_id %in% WA_COND_control_summary$plot_id
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    condition = if_else(
      plot_id %in% WA_prepost_burned_plot_id$plot_id,
      "burned",
      "control"
    ),
    fire_code = case_when(
      condition == "control" ~ "0",
      DSTRBCD1 %in% c(30, 31, 32) ~ as.character(DSTRBCD1),
      DSTRBCD2 %in% c(30, 31, 32) ~ as.character(DSTRBCD2),
      DSTRBCD3 %in% c(30, 31, 32) ~ as.character(DSTRBCD3),
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

# Confirm unique plot counts
WA_COND_treatment %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  summarise(n_unique_plots = n_distinct(plot_id))

# Pull coordinates for these plots
WA_PLOT_coordinates <- WA_PLOT_IDs %>%
  semi_join(WA_COND_treatment, by = "plot_id") %>%
  distinct(plot_id, LAT, LON)

# convert lat/long points in a spatial object
WA_PLOT_coordinates_vect <- vect(
  WA_PLOT_coordinates, geom = c("LON", "LAT"), crs = "EPSG:4326")

# extract climate for all lat/long coordinates (all unique plot IDs)
WA_terra_climate_values <- terra::extract(
  terra_climate_stack,
  WA_PLOT_coordinates_vect
) %>%
  bind_cols(
    WA_PLOT_coordinates %>% select(plot_id, LAT, LON)
  ) %>%
  select(plot_id, LAT, LON, everything(), -ID)

WA_terra_climate_values <- WA_terra_climate_values %>%
  left_join(
    WA_COND_treatment %>%
      select(plot_id, condition, fire_code),
    by = "plot_id"
  )

points(
  WA_PLOT_coordinates_vect,
  col = ifelse(WA_terra_climate_values$condition == "burned", "red", "blue"),
  pch = 19
)

ggplot(
  WA_terra_climate_values,
  aes(
    x = LON,
    y = LAT,
    color = def_mean
  )
) +
  geom_point(
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(
    name = "Climatic water deficit\n(mm)"
  ) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_classic()
```

## climate and distance matrices
```{r}
WA_climate_standardized <- WA_terra_climate_values %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(climate_variables), ~ as.numeric(scale(.)), .names = "z_{.col}"))

WA_burned_climate  <- WA_climate_standardized %>% filter(condition == "burned")
WA_control_climate <- WA_climate_standardized %>% filter(condition == "control")

WA_control_climate_cleaned <- WA_control_climate %>% 
  filter(if_all(all_of(c(z_vars, "LAT", "LON")), ~ !is.na(.)))

WA_climate_distance_matrix <- rdist(
  as.matrix(WA_burned_climate[, z_vars]),
  as.matrix(WA_control_climate_cleaned[, z_vars])
)
dimnames(WA_climate_distance_matrix) <- list(WA_burned_climate$plot_id, WA_control_climate_cleaned$plot_id)

WA_geo_dist_mat <- distm(
  as.matrix(WA_burned_climate[, c("LON", "LAT")]),
  as.matrix(WA_control_climate_cleaned[, c("LON", "LAT")]),
  fun = distHaversine
) / 1000

dimnames(WA_geo_dist_mat) <- list(WA_burned_climate$plot_id, WA_control_climate_cleaned$plot_id)

WA_clim_norm <- norm01(WA_climate_distance_matrix)
WA_geo_norm  <- norm01(WA_geo_dist_mat)

WA_combined_dist <- w_clim * norm01(WA_climate_distance_matrix) +
  (1 - w_clim) * norm01(WA_geo_dist_mat)

n_match <- 5

WA_match_list <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(WA_combined_dist)), function(i) {
  ord <- order(WA_combined_dist[i, ])
  top <- ord[seq_len(n_match)]
  data.frame(
    burned_id = WA_burned_climate$plot_id[i],
    control_id = WA_control_climate$plot_id[top],
    combined_distance = WA_combined_dist[i, top],
    climate_distance = WA_climate_distance_matrix[i, top],
    geo_distance_km = WA_geo_dist_mat[i, top],
    rank = seq_len(n_match),
    row.names = NULL
  )
})

WA_matches <- bind_rows(WA_match_list)
head(WA_matches, 10)

WA_matches %>% count(control_id, sort = TRUE) %>% head(10)

WA_matched_controls <- WA_control_climate_cleaned %>% 
  filter(plot_id %in% WA_matches$control_id)

ggplot(WA_matches, aes(x = geo_distance_km, y = climate_distance)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
  labs(
    x = "Geographic distance (km)",
    y = "Climate distance (standardized)",
    title = "Climate distance vs. geographic distance for matched pairs"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

## without replacement
```{r}
WA_matches_no_replacement <- without_replacement(
  dist_mat = WA_combined_dist,
  clim_mat = WA_climate_distance_matrix,
  geo_mat  = WA_geo_dist_mat,
  n_match  = 3
)

WA_matches_no_replacement %>% count(burned_id) %>% filter(n < 3)
```

## species overlap
```{r}

WA_burned_and_control_plot_id <- unique(c(WA_matches_no_replacement$burned_id, WA_matches_no_replacement$control_id))

WA_TREE_IDs_filtered <- WA_TREE_IDs %>%
  filter(plot_id %in% WA_burned_and_control_plot_id)

WA_TREE_species <- WA_TREE_IDs_filtered %>%
  left_join(
    REF_SPECIES %>%
      mutate(species = paste(GENUS, SPECIES, sep = "_")) %>%
      select(SPCD, species),
    by = "SPCD"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    WA_COND_treatment %>% distinct(plot_id, condition),
    by = "plot_id"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    WA_prepost_fire_summary %>% distinct(plot_id, fire_year),
    by = "plot_id"
  )

WA_TREE_species_filtered <- WA_TREE_species %>%
  filter(
    condition == "burned" | condition == "control"
  )

WA_species_summary_combined <- WA_TREE_species_filtered %>%
  group_by(species) %>%
  summarise(
    total_abundance = n(),
    n_plots = n_distinct(plot_id),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    frequency = n_plots / n_distinct(WA_TREE_species_filtered$plot_id)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_abundance))

WA_species_summary_by_condition <- WA_TREE_species_filtered %>%
  group_by(condition, species) %>%
  summarise(
    total_abundance = n(),
    n_plots = n_distinct(plot_id),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  mutate(
    frequency = n_plots / n_distinct(WA_TREE_species_filtered$plot_id[WA_TREE_species_filtered$condition == condition])
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(condition, desc(total_abundance))

WA_top_75_species <- WA_species_summary_combined %>%
  mutate(
    abundance_rank = rank(-total_abundance),
    frequency_rank = rank(-frequency),
    combined_rank = (abundance_rank + frequency_rank) / 2
  ) %>%
  arrange(combined_rank) %>%
  slice_head(n = 75)

WA_TREE_species_filtered %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  summarise(n_species = n_distinct(species), .groups = "drop")

WA_all_species <- WA_TREE_species_filtered %>%
  distinct(species) %>%
  pull(species)

length(WA_all_species) # 34 species

# to calculate dominance (the number of indiviudals of that species in that plot) and frequency (commonness)  (the number of plots the species occurs in / total number of plots)

WA_tree_count <- WA_TREE_species_filtered %>%
  group_by(plot_id) %>%
  summarise(total_trees_in_plot = n(), .groups = "drop")

WA_species_dominance <- WA_TREE_species_filtered %>%
  group_by(plot_id, species) %>%
  summarise(species_count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(WA_tree_count, by = "plot_id") %>%
  mutate(relative_abundance = species_count / total_trees_in_plot)

WA_species_summary <- WA_species_dominance %>%
  group_by(species) %>%
  summarise(
    n_plots_present  = n_distinct(plot_id),
    avg_dominance    = mean(relative_abundance),
    median_dominance = median(relative_abundance),
    min_dominance    = min(relative_abundance),
    max_dominance    = max(relative_abundance),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(frequency = n_plots_present / n_distinct(WA_TREE_species_filtered$plot_id)) %>%
  arrange(desc(frequency))

# is the number of species recruiting in control plots greater than burned (WA)
WA_SEEDLING_condition_filtered <- WA_SEEDLING_IDs %>%
  filter(plot_id %in% WA_burned_and_control_plot_id) %>%
  left_join(
    WA_COND_treatment %>% distinct(plot_id, condition),
    by = "plot_id"
  )

WA_SEEDLING_species_summary_condition <- WA_SEEDLING_condition_filtered %>%
  group_by(plot_id, condition, SPCD) %>%
  summarise(n_seedlings = sum(TREECOUNT, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(REF_SPECIES %>% dplyr::select(SPCD, GENUS, SPECIES), by = "SPCD") %>%
  mutate(species = paste(GENUS, SPECIES, sep = "_"))

WA_SEEDLING_plot_summary_condition <- WA_SEEDLING_species_summary_condition %>%
  group_by(plot_id, condition) %>%
  summarise(
    n_seedling_species = n_distinct(SPCD),
    n_seedlings = sum(n_seedlings, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

WA_SEEDLING_plot_summary_condition %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n_seedling_species, fill = condition)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, color = "white", position = "identity", alpha = 0.6) +
  facet_wrap(~condition, ncol = 1) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "Number of seedling species per plot",
    y = "Number of plots",
    title = "Seedling species richness: burned vs. control (WA)"
  )

WA_SEEDLING_plot_summary_condition %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = condition, y = n_seedling_species, fill = condition)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Number of seedling species per plot",
    title = "Seedling species richness: burned vs. control (WA)"
  )

WA_seedling_richness_summary_by_condition <- WA_SEEDLING_plot_summary_condition %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_richness   = mean(n_seedling_species),
    median_richness = median(n_seedling_species),
    n_plots         = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

WA_seedling_richness_summary_by_condition

WA_SEEDLING_species_summary_condition %>%
  group_by(species, condition, plot_id) %>%
  summarise(n_seedlings = sum(n_seedlings), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(species, condition) %>%
  summarise(mean_per_plot = mean(n_seedlings), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  slice_max(mean_per_plot, n = 20) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder_within(species, mean_per_plot, condition), y = mean_per_plot, fill = condition)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_x_reordered() +
  facet_wrap(~condition, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = "species",
    y = "average # seedlings per plot",
    title = "Average seedling abundance per species per plot: burned vs. control (WA)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

WA_SEEDLING_species_summary_condition %>%
  group_by(plot_id, condition, species) %>%
  summarise(n_seedlings = sum(n_seedlings), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(n_seedlings_capped = pmin(n_seedlings, 50)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = species, y = n_seedlings_capped, fill = condition)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6) +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_wrap(~condition, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = "species",
    y = "seedlings per plot (capped at 50)",
    title = "Seedling abundance per species per plot: burned vs. control (WA)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

summary(WA_SEEDLING_plot_summary_condition$n_seedling_species[WA_SEEDLING_plot_summary_condition$condition == "burned"])
summary(WA_SEEDLING_plot_summary_condition$n_seedling_species[WA_SEEDLING_plot_summary_condition$condition == "control"])

```

## whole plot network
```{r}

# filter CA_COND_IDs for forest types only, no disturbance filtering
WA_COND_forest_type <- WA_COND_IDs %>%
  filter(FORTYPCD %in% c(201, 202, 221, 222, 224, 225, 226, 241, 261, 262, 263, 264, 265, 267, 268, 270, 271, 281, 301, 304, 305, 321, 341, 342, 361, 365, 371, 722, 911, 912, 922, 923, 933, 941, 961))
         
         
(371, 201, 221, 222, 224, 225, 261, 922))

# get the unique plot_ids that qualify
WA_forest_type_plot_id <- WA_COND_forest_type %>%
  distinct(plot_id) %>%
  pull(plot_id)

length(WA_forest_type_plot_id)

# filter CA_PLOT_IDs for those plot_ids
WA_PLOT_forest_type <- WA_PLOT_IDs %>%
  filter(plot_id %in% WA_forest_type_plot_id)

# filter CA_TREE_IDs for those plot_ids
WA_TREE_forest_type <- WA_TREE_IDs %>%
  filter(plot_id %in% WA_forest_type_plot_id)

nrow(WA_PLOT_forest_type)
n_distinct(WA_TREE_forest_type$plot_id)

WA_TREE_forest_type_species <- WA_TREE_forest_type %>%
  left_join(
    REF_SPECIES %>%
      mutate(species = paste(GENUS, SPECIES, sep = "_")) %>%
      select(SPCD, species),
    by = "SPCD"
  )
sum(is.na(WA_TREE_forest_type_species$species))
n_distinct(WA_TREE_forest_type_species$species) # 49 species

# how different are the species from this workflow and the previous control plot filtering by climate?

# species from your burned/control workflow (already built)
WA_all_species   # from earlier: distinct species across CA_TREE_species_filtered

# species from your new forest-type-only workflow
WA_forest_type_all_species <- WA_TREE_forest_type_species %>%
  distinct(species) %>%
  pull(species)

length(WA_forest_type_all_species)

# species found in both workflows
intersect(WA_all_species, WA_forest_type_all_species)

# species found only in the burned/control workflow
setdiff(WA_all_species, WA_forest_type_all_species)

# species found only in the forest-type-only workflow
setdiff(WA_forest_type_all_species, WA_all_species) # 15 species more


# make sure the species coming out of SEEDLING match TREE
# filter CA_SEEDLING_IDs for those plot_ids
WA_SEEDLING_forest_type <- WA_SEEDLING_IDs %>%
  filter(plot_id %in% WA_forest_type_plot_id)

WA_SEEDLING_forest_type_species <- WA_SEEDLING_forest_type %>%
  left_join(
    REF_SPECIES %>%
      mutate(species = paste(GENUS, SPECIES, sep = "_")) %>%
      select(SPCD, species),
    by = "SPCD"
  )

# species list from seedling data (forest-type-only workflow)
WA_forest_type_all_seedling_species <- WA_SEEDLING_forest_type_species %>%
  distinct(species) %>%
  pull(species)

length(WA_forest_type_all_species)
length(WA_forest_type_all_seedling_species)

# species found in both tree and seedling data
intersect(WA_forest_type_all_species, WA_forest_type_all_seedling_species)

# species found only in tree data (mature trees, no seedlings recorded)
setdiff(WA_forest_type_all_species, WA_forest_type_all_seedling_species)

# species found only in seedling data (recruiting, but no mature trees recorded)
setdiff(WA_forest_type_all_seedling_species, WA_forest_type_all_species)

# create a shared species list
WA_forest_type_all_species
WA_forest_type_all_seedling_species

WA_species <- data.frame(
  species = unique(c(
    WA_forest_type_all_species,
    WA_forest_type_all_seedling_species
  ))
)

CA_OR_WA_forest_type_species_lists <- list(
  CA = CA_species,
  OR = OR_species,
  WA = WA_species
)

# species found in ALL THREE states
CA_OR_WA_forest_type_species <- Reduce(intersect, CA_OR_WA_forest_type_species_lists)
length(unique(CA_OR_WA_forest_type_species$species)) 
# 40 species shared in all 3 states

# total unique species across all three states combined
CA_OR_WA_forest_type_species_overlap <- Reduce(union, CA_OR_WA_forest_type_species_lists)
length(CA_OR_WA_forest_type_species_overlap$species) 
# 100 total unique species across all three states

write.csv(CA_OR_WA_forest_type_species_overlap, "updated_species_list_07182026.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# species found in exactly one state only (not shared with either other state)
length(setdiff(CA_species$species, union(OR_species$species, WA_species$species))) # 21 in CA only
length(setdiff(OR_species$species, union(CA_species$species, WA_species$species))) # 11 in OR only
length(setdiff(WA_species$species, union(CA_species$species, OR_species$species))) # 5 in WA only

# Shared by CA and OR, but NOT WA
setdiff(intersect(CA_species$species, OR_species$species), WA_species$species)
# 15 species

# Shared by CA and WA, but NOT OR
setdiff(intersect(CA_species$species, WA_species$species), OR_species$species)
# 1 species

# Shared by OR and WA, but NOT CA
setdiff(intersect(OR_species$species, WA_species$species), CA_species$species)
# 7 species 

# 100 unique species across all states, 40 shared and 37 unique

# Install and load the package if you haven't already
install.packages("ggvenn")
library(ggvenn)

# Combine your separate state lists into a single named list
venn_data <- list(
  California = CA_species$species,
  Oregon     = OR_species$species,
  Washington = WA_species$species
)

# Plot the diagram
ggvenn(venn_data, fill_color = c("#3A86FF", "#FF006E", "#FFBE0B"))

```


# CA, WA, OR species list

```{r}
CA_OR_WA_species_lists <- list(
  CA = CA_all_species,
  OR = OR_all_species,
  WA = WA_all_species
)

# species found in ALL THREE states
CA_OR_WA_species <- Reduce(intersect, CA_OR_WA_species_lists)
length(CA_OR_WA_species)

# total unique species across all three states combined
CA_OR_WA_species_overlap <- Reduce(union, CA_OR_WA_species_lists)
length(CA_OR_WA_species_overlap)

# species found in exactly one state only (not shared with either other state)
length(setdiff(CA_all_species, union(OR_all_species, WA_all_species)))
length(setdiff(OR_all_species, union(CA_all_species, WA_all_species)))
length(setdiff(WA_all_species, union(CA_all_species, OR_all_species)))

# 78 unique species across all states, 48 shared and 30 unique


# are burned plot species much different than control plot species
# CA
CA_species_burned  <- CA_TREE_species_filtered %>% filter(condition == "burned")  %>% distinct(species) %>% pull(species)
CA_species_control <- CA_TREE_species_filtered %>% filter(condition == "control") %>% distinct(species) %>% pull(species)

length(CA_species_burned) # 49 species
length(CA_species_control) # 60 species
length(intersect(CA_species_burned, CA_species_control)) # 44 species shared
length(setdiff(CA_species_burned, CA_species_control)) # 5 in burned only
length(setdiff(CA_species_control, CA_species_burned)) # 16 in control-only

# OR
OR_species_burned  <- OR_TREE_species_filtered %>% filter(condition == "burned")  %>% distinct(species) %>% pull(species)
OR_species_control <- OR_TREE_species_filtered %>% filter(condition == "control") %>% distinct(species) %>% pull(species)

length(OR_species_burned) # 44 species
length(OR_species_control) # 51 species
length(intersect(OR_species_burned, OR_species_control)) # 42 species shared
length(setdiff(OR_species_burned, OR_species_control)) # 2 in burned only
length(setdiff(OR_species_control, OR_species_burned)) # 9 in control only

# WA
WA_species_burned  <- WA_TREE_species_filtered %>% filter(condition == "burned")  %>% distinct(species) %>% pull(species)
WA_species_control <- WA_TREE_species_filtered %>% filter(condition == "control") %>% distinct(species) %>% pull(species)

length(WA_species_burned) # 25 species
length(WA_species_control) # 31 species
length(intersect(WA_species_burned, WA_species_control)) # 23 species shared
length(setdiff(WA_species_burned, WA_species_control)) # 2 in burned only
length(setdiff(WA_species_control, WA_species_burned)) # 8 in control only 
```


# species list
```{r}
# now lets aggregate a species list for each state
# STATE_TREE links to STAT_COND via the common identifier PLT_CN
# take the PLT_CN codes from forest_type_combined and filter STATE_TREE
CA_TREE_fire_only <- CA_TREE %>%
  filter(PLT_CN %in% CA_forest_type_combined$PLT_CN)

WA_TREE_fire_only <- WA_TREE %>%
  filter(PLT_CN %in% WA_forest_type_combined$PLT_CN)

OR_TREE_fire_only <- OR_TREE %>%
  filter(PLT_CN %in% OR_forest_type_combined$PLT_CN)

ID_TREE_fire_only <- ID_TREE %>%
  filter(PLT_CN %in% ID_forest_type_combined$PLT_CN)

MT_TREE_fire_only <- MT_TREE %>%
  filter(PLT_CN %in% MT_forest_type_combined$PLT_CN)

CO_TREE_fire_only <- CO_TREE %>%
  filter(PLT_CN %in% CO_forest_type_combined$PLT_CN)

NV_TREE_fire_only <- NV_TREE %>%
  filter(PLT_CN %in% NV_forest_type_combined$PLT_CN)

UT_TREE_fire_only <- UT_TREE %>%
  filter(PLT_CN %in% UT_forest_type_combined$PLT_CN)

WY_TREE_fire_only <- WY_TREE %>%
  filter(PLT_CN %in% WY_forest_type_combined$PLT_CN)

# attach SCIENTIFIC_NAME from REF_SPECIES to SPCD in CA_TREE_fire_only_spp_ID
CA_TREE_fire_only_spp_ID <- CA_TREE_fire_only %>%
  left_join(REF_SPECIES %>% dplyr::select(SPCD, SCIENTIFIC_NAME), by = "SPCD")

WA_TREE_fire_only_spp_ID <- WA_TREE_fire_only %>%
  left_join(REF_SPECIES %>% dplyr::select(SPCD, SCIENTIFIC_NAME), by = "SPCD")

OR_TREE_fire_only_spp_ID <- OR_TREE_fire_only %>%
  left_join(REF_SPECIES %>% dplyr::select(SPCD, SCIENTIFIC_NAME), by = "SPCD")

ID_TREE_fire_only_spp_ID <- ID_TREE_fire_only %>%
  left_join(REF_SPECIES %>% dplyr::select(SPCD, SCIENTIFIC_NAME), by = "SPCD")

MT_TREE_fire_only_spp_ID <- MT_TREE_fire_only %>%
  left_join(REF_SPECIES %>% dplyr::select(SPCD, SCIENTIFIC_NAME), by = "SPCD")

CO_TREE_fire_only_spp_ID <- CO_TREE_fire_only %>%
  left_join(REF_SPECIES %>% dplyr::select(SPCD, SCIENTIFIC_NAME), by = "SPCD")

NV_TREE_fire_only_spp_ID <- NV_TREE_fire_only %>%
  left_join(REF_SPECIES %>% dplyr::select(SPCD, SCIENTIFIC_NAME), by = "SPCD")

UT_TREE_fire_only_spp_ID <- UT_TREE_fire_only %>%
  left_join(REF_SPECIES %>% dplyr::select(SPCD, SCIENTIFIC_NAME), by = "SPCD")

WY_TREE_fire_only_spp_ID <- WY_TREE_fire_only %>%
  left_join(REF_SPECIES %>% dplyr::select(SPCD, SCIENTIFIC_NAME), by = "SPCD")

# create a list of tree species in fire only plots
CA_TREE_fire_only_spp_list <- unique(CA_TREE_fire_only_spp_ID$SCIENTIFIC_NAME)

WA_TREE_fire_only_spp_list <- unique(WA_TREE_fire_only_spp_ID$SCIENTIFIC_NAME)

OR_TREE_fire_only_spp_list <- unique(OR_TREE_fire_only_spp_ID$SCIENTIFIC_NAME)

ID_TREE_fire_only_spp_list <- unique(ID_TREE_fire_only_spp_ID$SCIENTIFIC_NAME)

MT_TREE_fire_only_spp_list <- unique(MT_TREE_fire_only_spp_ID$SCIENTIFIC_NAME)

CO_TREE_fire_only_spp_list <- unique(CO_TREE_fire_only_spp_ID$SCIENTIFIC_NAME)

NV_TREE_fire_only_spp_list <- unique(NV_TREE_fire_only_spp_ID$SCIENTIFIC_NAME)

UT_TREE_fire_only_spp_list <- unique(UT_TREE_fire_only_spp_ID$SCIENTIFIC_NAME)

WY_TREE_fire_only_spp_list <- unique(WY_TREE_fire_only_spp_ID$SCIENTIFIC_NAME)

# can we now take the species lists for each state and find the intersection?
#(i.e. we want a list of total unique species across states)

# merge all states species lists and keep only unique species
total_unique_species <- unique(c(
  CA_TREE_fire_only_spp_list,
  WA_TREE_fire_only_spp_list,
  OR_TREE_fire_only_spp_list,
  ID_TREE_fire_only_spp_list,
  MT_TREE_fire_only_spp_list,
  CO_TREE_fire_only_spp_list,
  NV_TREE_fire_only_spp_list,
  UT_TREE_fire_only_spp_list,
  WY_TREE_fire_only_spp_list
))

write.csv(total_unique_species, file = "fire-severity-unique-species-list.csv")
```


# plot locations/distribution
```{r}
# try plotting locations on a map of the US
# color code by burn severity to see the spatial distribution of high and low severity

#extract plot locations from CA_PLOT or 'CA' for the PLT_CN's on CA_fire_only

CA_burned_plot_coordinates <- CA_TREE_fire_only %>%
  left_join(
    CA %>% dplyr::select(CN, LAT, LON),
    by = c("PLT_CN" = "CN")
  )


# Convert to sf object using LAT and LON columns
CA_burned_sf <- st_as_sf(
  CA_burned_plot_coordinates,
  coords = c("LON", "LAT"),  # Order matters: (lon, lat)
  crs = 4326  # WGS84 coordinate system
)

# Get US states shapefile
usa_shapefile <- ne_states(country = "United States of America", returnclass = "sf")

# Filter for western states
western_states <- usa_shapefile %>%
  filter(name %in% c(
    "California", "Oregon", "Washington", "Nevada", "Idaho", "Utah",
    "Arizona", "New Mexico", "Montana", "Wyoming", "Colorado"
  ))


# Join disturbance code into your plots dataframe
CA_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1 <- CA_burned_plot_coordinates %>%
  left_join(
    forest_type_combined %>% dplyr::select(PLT_CN, DSTRBCD1, DSTRBYR1),
    by = "PLT_CN"
  )

# Convert to sf with disturbance code included
CA_burned_sf <- st_as_sf(
  CA_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1,
  coords = c("LON", "LAT"),
  crs = 4326
)

ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = western_states, fill = "gray95", color = "black") +
  geom_sf(
    data = CA_burned_sf %>% filter(DSTRBCD1 %in% c(31, 32)),
    aes(color = factor(DSTRBCD1)),
    size = 1, alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("31" = "orange", "32" = "red"),
    labels = c("31" = "Disturbance 31", "32" = "Disturbance 32"),
    name = "Disturbance Code"
  ) +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-125, -100), ylim = c(30, 45)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Burned plots in low-moderate and high severity",
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude"
  )

# lets add other states now

# 1. Join coordinates from the state-level plot table
OR_burned_plot_coordinates <- OR_TREE_fire_only %>%
  left_join(
    OR %>% dplyr::select(CN, LAT, LON),
    by = c("PLT_CN" = "CN")
  )

# 2. Join in the disturbance code from forest_type_combined
OR_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1 <- OR_burned_plot_coordinates %>%
  left_join(
    OR_forest_type_combined %>% dplyr::select(PLT_CN, DSTRBCD1, DSTRBYR1),
    by = "PLT_CN"
  )

# 3. Convert to sf object with disturbance info
OR_burned_sf <- st_as_sf(
  OR_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1,
  coords = c("LON", "LAT"),
  crs = 4326
)


# 1. Join coordinates from the state-level plot table
WA_burned_plot_coordinates <- WA_TREE_fire_only %>%
  left_join(
    WA %>% dplyr::select(CN, LAT, LON),
    by = c("PLT_CN" = "CN")
  )

# 2. Join in the disturbance code from forest_type_combined
WA_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1 <- WA_burned_plot_coordinates %>%
  left_join(
    WA_forest_type_combined %>% dplyr::select(PLT_CN, DSTRBCD1, DSTRBYR1),
    by = "PLT_CN"
  )

# 3. Convert to sf object with disturbance info
WA_burned_sf <- st_as_sf(
  WA_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1,
  coords = c("LON", "LAT"),
  crs = 4326
)

# 1. Join coordinates from the state-level plot table
ID_burned_plot_coordinates <- ID_TREE_fire_only %>%
  left_join(
    ID %>% dplyr::select(CN, LAT, LON),
    by = c("PLT_CN" = "CN")
  )

# 2. Join in the disturbance code from forest_type_combined
ID_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1 <- ID_burned_plot_coordinates %>%
  left_join(
    ID_forest_type_combined %>% dplyr::select(PLT_CN, DSTRBCD1),
    by = "PLT_CN"
  )

# 3. Convert to sf object with disturbance info
ID_burned_sf <- st_as_sf(
  ID_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1,
  coords = c("LON", "LAT"),
  crs = 4326
)


# 1. Join coordinates from the state-level plot table
MT_burned_plot_coordinates <- MT_TREE_fire_only %>%
  left_join(
    MT %>% dplyr::select(CN, LAT, LON),
    by = c("PLT_CN" = "CN")
  )

# 2. Join in the disturbance code from forest_type_combined
MT_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1 <- MT_burned_plot_coordinates %>%
  left_join(
    MT_forest_type_combined %>% dplyr::select(PLT_CN, DSTRBCD1),
    by = "PLT_CN"
  )

# 3. Convert to sf object with disturbance info
MT_burned_sf <- st_as_sf(
  MT_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1,
  coords = c("LON", "LAT"),
  crs = 4326
)

# 1. Join coordinates from the state-level plot table
CO_burned_plot_coordinates <- CO_TREE_fire_only %>%
  left_join(
    CO %>% dplyr::select(CN, LAT, LON),
    by = c("PLT_CN" = "CN")
  )

# 2. Join in the disturbance code from forest_type_combined
CO_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1 <- CO_burned_plot_coordinates %>%
  left_join(
    CO_forest_type_combined %>% dplyr::select(PLT_CN, DSTRBCD1),
    by = "PLT_CN"
  )

# 3. Convert to sf object with disturbance info
CO_burned_sf <- st_as_sf(
  CO_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1,
  coords = c("LON", "LAT"),
  crs = 4326
)

# 1. Join coordinates from the state-level plot table
UT_burned_plot_coordinates <- UT_TREE_fire_only %>%
  left_join(
    UT %>% dplyr::select(CN, LAT, LON),
    by = c("PLT_CN" = "CN")
  )

# 2. Join in the disturbance code from forest_type_combined
UT_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1 <- UT_burned_plot_coordinates %>%
  left_join(
    UT_forest_type_combined %>% dplyr::select(PLT_CN, DSTRBCD1),
    by = "PLT_CN"
  )

# 3. Convert to sf object with disturbance info
UT_burned_sf <- st_as_sf(
  UT_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1,
  coords = c("LON", "LAT"),
  crs = 4326
)


# 1. Join coordinates from the state-level plot table
WY_burned_plot_coordinates <- WY_TREE_fire_only %>%
  left_join(
    WY %>% dplyr::select(CN, LAT, LON),
    by = c("PLT_CN" = "CN")
  )

# 2. Join in the disturbance code from forest_type_combined
WY_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1 <- WY_burned_plot_coordinates %>%
  left_join(
    WY_forest_type_combined %>% dplyr::select(PLT_CN, DSTRBCD1),
    by = "PLT_CN"
  )

# 3. Convert to sf object with disturbance info
WY_burned_sf <- st_as_sf(
  WY_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1,
  coords = c("LON", "LAT"),
  crs = 4326
)

###### this chunk for NV didnt work, see below instead
# 1. Join coordinates from the state-level plot table
NV_burned_plot_coordinates <- NV_TREE_fire_only %>%
  left_join(
    NV %>% select(CN, LAT, LON),
    by = c("PLT_CN" = "CN")
  )

# 2. Join in the disturbance code from forest_type_combined
NV_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1 <- NV_burned_plot_coordinates %>%
  left_join(
    NV_forest_type_combined %>% select(PLT_CN, DSTRBCD1),
    by = "PLT_CN"
  )

# 3. Convert to sf object with disturbance info
NV_burned_sf <- st_as_sf(
  NV_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1,
  coords = c("LON", "LAT"),
  crs = 4326
)

######
NV_fire_coords <- NV_fire_only %>%
  left_join(NV %>% dplyr::select(CN, LAT, LON), by = c("PLT_CN" = "CN"))

NV_fire_sf <- st_as_sf(NV_fire_coords, coords = c("LON", "LAT"), crs = 4326)



NV_burned_plot_coordinates <- NV_fire_only %>%
  left_join(NV %>% dplyr::select(CN, LAT, LON), by = c("PLT_CN" = "CN"))

# Step 3: Convert to sf object
NV_burned_sf <- st_as_sf(NV_burned_plot_coordinates, coords = c("LON", "LAT"), crs = 4326)

normalize_types <- function(x) {
  dplyr::mutate(
    x,
    dplyr::across(
      where(~ !inherits(.x, "sfc")),
      as.character
    )
  )
}



all_burned_sf <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  normalize_types(CA_burned_sf),
  normalize_types(OR_burned_sf),
  normalize_types(WA_burned_sf),
  normalize_types(ID_burned_sf),
  normalize_types(MT_burned_sf),
  normalize_types(CO_burned_sf),
  normalize_types(UT_burned_sf),
  normalize_types(NV_burned_sf),
  normalize_types(WY_burned_sf)
)




ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = western_states, fill = "gray95", color = "black") +
  geom_sf(
    data = all_burned_sf %>% filter(DSTRBCD1 %in% c(31, 32)),
    aes(color = factor(DSTRBCD1)),
    size = 1, alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("31" = "orange", "32" = "red"),
    name = "Disturbance Code"
  ) +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-125, -100), ylim = c(30, 50)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude"
  )
```
# plot level summary stats
```{r}
# lets visualize some summary stats about our burned and unburned plots

h1 <- ggplot(
  data = CA_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1 %>%
    filter(DSTRBYR1 <= 2025) %>%
    group_by(PLT_CN) %>%
    slice_min(DSTRBYR1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup(),  # keep one row per unique plot
  aes(x = DSTRBYR1)
) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, boundary = 1990, color = "black", fill = "steelblue") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1990, 2025, by = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Histogram of Disturbance Years in CA (using first year fire was recorded)",
    x = "Disturbance Year",
    y = "Count (Unique PLT_CN)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))

h1

h2 <- ggplot(
  data = OR_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1 %>%
    filter(DSTRBYR1 >= 1990, DSTRBYR1 <= 2025) %>%
    group_by(PLT_CN) %>%
    slice_min(DSTRBYR1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup(),
  aes(x = DSTRBYR1)
) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, boundary = 1990, color = "black", fill = "steelblue") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1990, 2025, by = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Histogram of Disturbance Years in OR (using first year fire was recorded)",
    x = "Disturbance Year",
    y = "Count (Unique Plots)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))


h2



h3 <- ggplot(
  data = WA_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1 %>%
    filter(DSTRBYR1 <= 2025) %>%
    group_by(PLT_CN) %>%
    slice_min(DSTRBYR1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup(),  # keep one row per unique plot
  aes(x = DSTRBYR1)
) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, boundary = 1990, color = "black", fill = "steelblue") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1990, 2025, by = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Histogram of Disturbance Years in WA (using first year fire was recorded)",
    x = "Disturbance Year",
    y = "Count (Unique Plots)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))



h3
grid.arrange (h1, h2, h3)

h4 <- ggplot(
  data = CA_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1 %>%
    filter(!is.na(DSTRBYR1), DSTRBYR1 >= 1990, DSTRBYR1 <= 2025) %>%
    group_by(PLT_CN) %>%
    slice_min(DSTRBYR1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(TimeSinceFire = 2025 - DSTRBYR1),
  aes(x = TimeSinceFire)
) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, boundary = 0, color = "black", fill = "darkorange") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 35, by = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "CA",
    x = "Time Since Fire (Years)",
    y = "Count (Unique Plots)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
h4

h5 <- ggplot(
  data = OR_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1 %>%
    filter(!is.na(DSTRBYR1), DSTRBYR1 >= 1990, DSTRBYR1 <= 2025) %>%
    group_by(PLT_CN) %>%
    slice_min(DSTRBYR1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(TimeSinceFire = 2025 - DSTRBYR1),
  aes(x = TimeSinceFire)
) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, boundary = 0, color = "black", fill = "darkorange") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 35, by = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "OR",
    x = "Time Since Fire (Years)",
    y = "Count (Unique Plots)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

h5

h6 <- ggplot(
  data = WA_burned_plot_coordinates_DSTRBCD1 %>%
    filter(!is.na(DSTRBYR1), DSTRBYR1 >= 1990, DSTRBYR1 <= 2025) %>%
    group_by(PLT_CN) %>%
    slice_min(DSTRBYR1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(TimeSinceFire = 2025 - DSTRBYR1),
  aes(x = TimeSinceFire)
) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, boundary = 0, color = "black", fill = "darkorange") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 35, by = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "WA",
    x = "Time Since Fire (Years)",
    y = "Count (Unique Plots)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

h6

grid.arrange (h4, h5, h6)


# 600+ plots in ground fire and 400+ plots in canopy fire in CA
ggplot(dplyr::filter(CA_cond, DSTRBCD1 %in% c(30, 31, 32)),
       aes(x = factor(DSTRBCD1))) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(x = "Disturbance Code", y = "Count",
       title = "Fire-Related Disturbances (Codes 30–32)") +
  theme_minimal()
```

# climate extraction 
```{r}
```

# understory
```{r}
# lets look at the understory too...

# attach SCIENTIFIC_NAME from REF_SPECIES to CA_veg_understory_fire_only by matching VEG_SPCD
# to SPECIES_SYMBOL
CA_veg_understory_fire_only_spp_ID <- CA_veg_understory_fire_only %>%
  left_join(REF_SPECIES %>%
              select(SPECIES_SYMBOL, SCIENTIFIC_NAME),
            by = c("VEG_SPCD" = "SPECIES_SYMBOL")) ## this has the be redone, only gives trees

# take the PLT_CN codes from forest_type_combined and filter CA_veg_understory to those PLT_CN's
CA_veg_understory <- CA_P2VEG_SUBPLOT_SPP

CA_veg_understory_fire_only <- CA_veg_understory %>%
  filter(PLT_CN %in% forest_type_combined$PLT_CN)

# CA_veg_understory_fire_only uses VEG_SPCD instead of SPCD



# create a list of understory species in fire only plots



# how many resurveys do fire only mixed conifer plots in CA have?
#CA_PLOT (or just CA here) links to CA_COND via the common identifier PLT_CN


#If INVYR (plot visit year) < DSTRBYR1 → then this visit happened before the fire.

#If INVYR > DSTRBYR1 → then this visit was after the fire.

#If equal, the fire occurred in the same year as the plot visit

# if we want to link CA_PLOT (or CA) with CA_cond that has been filtered by disturbance codes
# and forest types then we want to lin forest_type_combined with CA_PLOT or CA but PLT_CN in
#CA_PLOT of CA PLT_CN is the same as CN

inventory_year <- CA_cond %>%
  filter(DSTRBCD1 %in% c(31,32) | DSTRBCD2 %in% c(31,32) | DSTRBCD3 %in% c(31,32)) %>%
  left_join(CA, by = "PLT_CN") %>%
  mutate(fire_year = coalesce(DSTRBYR1, DSTRBYR2, DSTRBYR3),
         pre_fire = INVYR < fire_year,
         post_fire  = INVYR > fire_year)


resurveyed_fire_only_plots <- CA_TREE_fire_only %>%
  group_by(PLT_CN) %>%
  summarise(n_years = n_distinct(INVYR))
# all plots only have 1 visit?

resurveyed_fire_only_plots <- CA_TREE_fire_only %>%
  group_by(PLT_CN) %>%
  summarise(INVYR_list = list(sort(unique(INVYR))))


```
